segments.text:
MEMBRANE BOUND VESICLES
Proteins do NOT need to cross membranes
Transport between ER, Golgi, lysosomes, exterior

COATED VESICLES
3 main coat types
COPII: from ER to Golgi
COPI: within Golgi and Golgi to ER
Clathrin: between Golgi and cell membrane

3 main coat types
COPII: from ER to Golgi
COPI: within Golgi and Golgi to ER
Clathrin: between Golgi and cell membrane
Move proteins between things

VESICULAR MOVEMENT
Budding from donor membrane
Fusion with acceptor/target membrane
start
end

COPII COAT FORMATION 1
Regulated by SAR1 (small GTPase protein)
Sar1-GDP: soluble in cytosol
Sar1-GTP: membrane bound
Sec12: Sar1 GEF
Sar1
on(GTP) hydrophobic tail is free
off(GDP) hydrophobic tail in membrane
Sec 12 (secretory pathway
GEF guanine nucleotide exchange factor
- Promote Sar1 to release

COPII COAT FORMATION 2
Sar1-GTP recruits Sec23/24 (coat proteins)
Sec23/24 recruit Sec13/31(more coat proteins)
Coat proteins “curve” membrane
Sec23/24 bind cargo receptors

BUD FORMATION
Continued curvature by coat forms bud
Coat can form cage in vitro (without membrane)

VESICLE “PINCHING OFF”
COPI and COPII vesicles appear to not need “extra” step
For clathrin-coated vesicles membranes brought together by dynamin
Requires GTP hydrolysis
Clathrin
-

COPII VESICLE UNCOATING
Sar1 hydrolyzes GTP and releases from membrane
Coat disassembles

VESICLE TARGET SPECIFICITY
Vesicle needs to fuse with correct target
Regulated by Rabs and SNAREs

RAB PROTEINS
Specific for different types of membranes
>60 in humans
Rab-GTP: lipid tail bound to membrane
Rab-GDP: lipid tail masked (so cytosolic)

SNAREs
Integral membrane proteins
Dozens of types (each on different membranes)
V-SNARE: SNARE on vesicle
T-SNARE: SNARE on target membrane

VESICLE TARGET RECOGNITION
Rab-GTP binds to Rab effector
V-SNAREs and T-SNAREs bind

MEMBRANE FUSION
SNAREs pull membranes close enough for lipids to move between bilayers

RECYCLING OF SNAREs
Required to return V-SNAREs to donor membrane
NSF and ATP break V-SNARE/T-SNARE binding

2013 NOBEL PRIZE
Randy Schekman – Sec mutants
Jim Rothman – SNAREs, NSF, etc
Yeast mutants defective in secretion
Nerve cells releasing neurotransmitters
